rulings.Case Title: KERRY MANSFIELD VS. BROTHERS INTERNATIONAL HOLDING CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, March 10, 2014, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT WORLD GOLF TOUR, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication World Golf Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. World Golf Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to the first three causes of action as unopposed. The motion is denied as to the fourth cause of action for express indemnity. World Golf Inc. has not met its initial burden establishing as a matter of law it is not obligated to indemnify Brothers International Holding Corp. pursuant to the terms of the Lease Agreement, attached as Exhibit C to the Declaration of Matthew S. Conant. It is undisputed that Plaintiff Kerry Mansfield slipped and fell in the stairwell of the building. (Undisputed Material Fact ("UMF"), no. 1.) It is further undisputed that Ms. Mansfield was an employee of World Golf Inc. at the time of the accident. (UMF no. 2.) The indemnification provision of the Lease Agreement provides in pertinent part that "[World Golf Inc.] shall hold [Brothers International Holding Corp.] ¿harmless from and indemnify [it] against any claims, liability damages, costs or expenses¿to the extent arising from (a) the acts or omissions of¿[World Golf Inc.'s] employees¿.in or about the Real Property." (Lease Agreement, par. 14(b).) "Real Property" is defined as the building and the land it sits on. (Lease Agreement, par. 1.) Here, the undisputed evidence establishes that the injury resulted from an act of World Golf Inc.'s employee while in the building. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: JANE ROE VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf Continued Deposition And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $10,000.00
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, March 10, 2014, Line 3, CROSS COMPLAINANTs DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, DEUTSCHE BANK AG and NICOLE DESANTIS's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Continued Deposition And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $10,000.00. Pro Tem Judge Elan Lambert, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RASHID ALATRAQCHI VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Defs' Responses To Pltf'S Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, March 10, 2014, Line 12, PLAINTIFF RASHID ALATRAQCHI'S MOTION To Compel Defs' Responses To Pltf'S Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions Denied. First, Plaintiff failed to file a separate statement attaching the defendants' responses to either the request for production of documents or the special interrogatories to which he is seeking answers. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1345(a).) Without such a statement the court has no basis on which to make a ruling. Second, Plaintiff failed to make a good faith attempt to meet and confer following receipt of Defendant counsel's meet and confer letter dated January 27, 2014. (Code Civ. Proc. §§ 2016.040, 2030.300(b), 2031.310(b)(2). See O'Connor Decl., 12, 13, Exhibit F.) Monetary sanctions of $ 1,500.00 are awarded in favor of Uber Technologies, Inc. and against Rashid Alatracqchi to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: HUA LIN VS. DALY CITY HB INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, March 10, 2014, Line 17, DEFENDANT DALY CITY HB DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) As to the breach of contract cause of action, the Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") alleges no specific facts as to how Defendant breached the Settlement Agreement or how any alleged breach caused damage to Plaintiff. As to the "exemplary damages" cause of action, the TAC appears to allege a cause of action for fraudulent concealment. The TAC does not allege facts establishing that Defendant concealed anything. The Settlement Agreement attached as Exhibit B to the TAC expressly states that the settlement would be reported as nonemployee compensation. (Par. 3(b).) The Form 1099-MISC attached to the TAC as Exhibit C shows that the compensation was reported as nonemployee compensation, in accordance with the terms of the Settlement Agreement. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend in light of the fact Plaintiff has had four opportunities to plead facts establishing a cause of action and has been unable to do so. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EG

rulings.Case Title: HUA LIN VS. DALY CITY HB INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, March 10, 2014, Line 17, DEFENDANT DALY CITY HB'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT Denied as moot in light of the ruling on demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than March 10, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). rulings.Case Title: BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY INC., A CORPORATION DBA VS. FLAMEZ GRILL LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, March 10, 2014, Line 21, PLAINTIFF BIRITE RESTAURANT SUPPLY INC., A CORPORATION DBA APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER Off calendar as untimely pursuant to CCP §§ 484.040 and 1005(b). The application was served on February 7, 2014 by substitute service and mailed on February 8, 2014. Service is deemed complete on the 10th day after mailing. (CCP § 415.20(b).) Plaintiff did not allow for 16 court days notice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/EHG
